title: [Bug]: Crash when removing a subfigure with a subplot in a figure
body:
### Bug summary

The program crashes with "AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute '_mouseover_set'" when I create a figure with subfigures, create subplots in the subfigures, and remove a subfigure. 

### Code for reproduction

```Python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig = plt.figure()
subfigs = fig.subfigures(1, 2)
subfigs[0].subplots()
subfigs[0].remove()
```

### Actual outcome

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[2], [line 8](vscode-notebook-cell:?execution_count=2&line=8)
      6 subfigs[0].subplots()
      7 subfigs[1].subplots()
----> [8](vscode-notebook-cell:?execution_count=2&line=8) subfigs[0].remove()

File ~/dataviz-render-update-testing/matplotlib/lib/matplotlib/artist.py:260, in Artist.remove(self)
    257 _ax_flag = False
    258 if hasattr(self, 'axes') and self.axes:
    259     # remove from the mouse hit list
--> [260](https://vscode-remote+ssh-002dremote-002bsccpu5-002ecse-002eust-002ehk.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/data/wluak/dataviz-render-update-testing/~/dataviz-render-update-testing/matplotlib/lib/matplotlib/artist.py:260)     self.axes._mouseover_set.discard(self)
    261     self.axes.stale = True
    262     self.axes = None  # decouple the artist from the Axes

AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute '_mouseover_set'

### Expected outcome

No crash.

### Additional information

_No response_

### Operating system

_No response_

### Matplotlib Version

0.2.0.dev53882+unknown.g607d2c380.g02b42db27

### Matplotlib Backend

_No response_

### Python version

_No response_

### Jupyter version

_No response_

### Installation

None
comments_text:
The problem is that we everything inherits from `Artist` (including the `Figure` family of clases), but the type of `obj.axes` is not stable across all classes: for the Figure family it is `list[Axes]`, for `Axes` it is `None`, and for everything else it is `None | Axes` [Yes this is non-ideal, no we would not do it this way if designing from scratch now, no it is not worth the deprecation cost to change it at this point].  Additionally we customize the `remove` functionality by setting a callback at run time.  Thus, the assumption in the base `Artist.remove` is that if the artists have a remove callback set and the `self.axes` attribute is not falsey, then we are in the third category so we can treat the `self.axes` as an Axes object and need to clean up `Axes` stuff that was set in `add_artist`.


Looking at this I see a couple of paths:
 - update the `_remove_method` callback for sub-figures to recursively remove their axes/subfigures so that by the time we hit the code is `Artist.remove` `self.axes == []`
 - update `Artist.remove` to bail on self.axes being a list
 - update `Artist.remove` to bail on Figure subclasses of self
 - update `Artist.romove` to only not bail on Axes of self.axes
 - add a custom `remove` method someplace in the Figure family

None of these are super great, but I think overriding the `remove` method is the worst choice (mixing methods of injecting logic always makes things worse).  Making sure `SubFigures` recursively clean them selves will make removing them a bit more expensive and will complicate some future plans but might help with releasing memory (breaks reference cycles) and is more consistent with the current behavior else where (e.g. this code block where we are making sure that for a data artist (`Line2D`) that is being removed from an Axes that they get fully decoupled).  Changing the test from the truthyness of `self.axes` to a type check + not None check is probably the minimal change that will fix the problem with the fewe

Oh, that's unfortunate. We actually have a test for `SubFigure` removal, but it's an _empty_ one:
https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/blob/dfa19f6fe43a9f4d9c5b51683a6125eb797e554d/lib/matplotlib/tests/test_figure.py#L1575-L1579
author: williamlus

title: [Bug]: Axes.cla() resets axis label position of twin axes
body:
### Bug summary

I'm making many plots in a loop using the same Figure and Axes. The basic setup including a twin Axes is done once and the Axes are cleared at the end of the loop using `cla()`.  

`Axes.cla()` resets the label position of the `yaxis` of the twin Axes to the default (left), resulting in misplaced ylabels in all but the first loops.  

Ticks and tick labels of the left y axis are kept at the correct side, so I would the axis label also expect to be kept at the correct side.

### Code for reproduction

```Python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots()
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
for i in range(2):
    # do some plotting here
    ax2.set(ylabel=f'ylabel of plot # {i}', ylim=(1, i + 2))
    fig.savefig(f'{i}.png')
    ax1.cla()
    ax2.cla()
```


### Actual outcome

**1.png:**

![1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f41d9cb4-b564-4480-b1f3-76d9b95c38f3)


### Expected outcome

The expected outcome was produced by adding `ax2.yaxis.set_label_position('right')` to the loop:  

![1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7970837a-71a3-4278-9afc-3f17ab7559a5)


### Additional information

_No response_

### Operating system

_No response_

### Matplotlib Version

3.9.2

### Matplotlib Backend

_No response_

### Python version

_No response_

### Jupyter version

_No response_

### Installation

None
comments_text:
I guess an straightforward fix would be to restore the label position after clearing the axis:

https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/blob/a254b687df97cda8c6affa37a1dfcf213f8e6c3a/lib/matplotlib/axes/_base.py#L1273-L1274

by changing line 1274 to

```python
old_label_position = axis.label_position
axis.clear()
axis.label_position = old_label_position
```

or better do it in Axis.clear() around:  

https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/blob/a254b687df97cda8c6affa37a1dfcf213f8e6c3a/lib/matplotlib/axis.py#L892
Semi-related: #27825 and the associated PR #28101
author: StefRe

title: [Bug]: Redundant month in ConciseDateFormatter offset text when it displays days and months
body:
### Bug summary

When you make a plot with the `ConciseDateFormatter` that shows on the x axis days and months the `offset_string` also shows the month (with the year)  which I think is redundant because you only need the year 





### Code for reproduction

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
import datetime

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

d1 = datetime.datetime(1997, 1, 1)
d2 = d1 + datetime.timedelta(weeks=520)

locator = mdates.AutoDateLocator()
formatter = mdates.ConciseDateFormatter(locator)
ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(locator)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(formatter)

ax.plot([d1, d2], [0, 0])

ax.set_xlim(d1, d1 + datetime.timedelta(weeks=3))
ax.set_xlim(d1 + datetime.timedelta(weeks=1),
                d1 + datetime.timedelta(weeks=10))

plt.show()
```


### Actual outcome

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1191676/212464462-14d44bf9-4cd4-44e8-8691-c51150ba1766.png)

### Expected outcome

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1191676/212464801-49494570-8d3e-421e-9a39-4de809048c45.png)

### Additional information

When the `ConciseDateFormatter` was proposed [here](https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/pull/10841) you can see the same expected behavior:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1191676/212465059-1d6bb107-4246-4562-877f-f6585d7b4fdf.png)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 22.04.1 LTS

### Matplotlib Version

3.6.2

### Matplotlib Backend

agg

### Python version

3.10.6

### Jupyter version

_No response_

### Installation

pip
comments_text:
According to the milestone, the `ConciseDateFormatter` went in at v3.1, and the example in the v3.1 gallery shows “2005-Apr” on the offset even though “Apr” is also an axes tick lebel.  So I think it has always been this way.
https://matplotlib.org/3.1.0/gallery/ticks_and_spines/date_concise_formatter.html#sphx-glr-gallery-ticks-and-spines-date-concise-formatter-py

This is expected behaviour - if you had another month in your date range, I think it would change to just showing the year.  If you want to change the formats for the different levels there is an *offset_formats* kwarg to `ConciseFormatter`, however, I think there are cases in this level where you would want the month to show up as well, so it probably should stay the same.    If you want, you can also manually change the offset text `ax.xaxis.offsetText.set_string('2009')`.  

It would be hard to argue that we should change the default, because that would require a whole deprecation cycle, which is difficult for kwargs, and I'd say not worth the pain in this case.

I contemplated this when working on https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/pull/22871

There a special casing was added for the year and I was thinking about the other cases as well. IIRC it really comes down to what the format strings include and if the first day of the month is hit or not. Although I agree that conceptually, the month should not be in your example, one would simply need another formatter for that, as combining modifiable static formatting and dynamic adaptation of the formatting is not really possible (either we always guess or never guess, sort of, the current solution never guesses).

I think that another formatter that prints the ticks at a suitable level and then print all remaining date information in the offset, as long as it is not already in the ticks would be doable though, possibly with an argument for "highest" level to be shown in the offset.

(Finally, one could question if it actually should be Mar in the example. It seems like maybe Jan is a more natural choice for offset?)
author: persep

title: [Bug]: Mask not taken into account for edges in pcolormesh()
body:
### Bug summary

Hello,
There seems to be a regression in `pcolormesh()`: the array mask is not taken into account for drawing the edges. An expected output is the graphic below, taken from the following web page (except that the color of the edges is wrong):
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63458863/way-to-contour-outer-edge-of-selected-grid-region-in-python
When I try to reproduce this graphic, I observe that the edges are drawn over the whole figure instead of being limited to a ring as in the link pointed above. Can it be corrected?
Thank you,
Olivier

### Code for reproduction

```python
from numpy import *
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

x = linspace(-pi/2, pi/2, 30)
y = linspace(-pi/2, pi/2, 30)
x,y = meshgrid(x,y)
z = sin(x**2+y**2)[:-1,:-1]
fig1, ax1 = plt.subplots()

ax1.pcolormesh(x,y,z)

rect = (z > 0.9)
rect = ma.masked_less(rect, 1)

ax1.pcolormesh(x, y, rect, facecolor='None', edgecolor='w', linewidth=0.5)

fig2, ax2 = plt.subplots()
ax2.pcolormesh(x, y, rect)
```


### Actual outcome

![test](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1868182/210155817-28759c52-f252-4fb3-9b26-94a011c16b2a.png)

### Expected outcome

![Fqbe6](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1868182/210155744-642a7ab7-db25-4954-87ba-0b3fba2628ee.png)

### Additional information

_No response_

### Operating system

_No response_

### Matplotlib Version

3.5.2, 3.6.2

### Matplotlib Backend

TkAgg

### Python version

Python 3.10.9

### Jupyter version

_No response_

### Installation

None
comments_text:
> 3.5.2, 3.6.2

You said it's a regression; are you saying it worked in 3.5.2 and broke in 3.6.2, or you only tested those two versions and it was broken in both?

It is broken in both 3.5.2 and 3.6.2. I copied the expected image from a StackOverflow question asked in August 2020 (see the link above). So, some time near that date, the mask was correctly taken into account for edges in pcolormesh().

This was changed in v3.4. `pcolor` is a better fit for applying the mask other properties since the masked cells simply aren't drawn with `pcolor`. Whereas with `pcolormesh` the cells are there, and thus now they get a solid edge color.

For your example, that would mean your edgecolor overlay would be written as: `ax1.pcolor(x, y, rect, facecolor='none', edgecolor='w', linewidth=0.5)`

There are some possible actions: this could be documented better, or perhaps someone could even write another method/helper function for matplotlib to draw collections of edges for a mesh (extending LineCollection).

title: [Bug]: Adding contour labels affects the shape of filled contours
body: ### Bug summary The shape of the filled contours changes unexpectedly when I add the contour labels by calling `axs[1].clabel(CS)`. ### Code for reproduction ```Python import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import numpy as np delta = 0.025 x = np.arange(-3.0, 3.0, delta) y = np.arange(-2.0, 2.0, delta) X, Y = np.meshgrid(x, y) Z1 = np.exp(-X**2 - Y**2) Z2 = np.exp(-(X - 1)**2 - (Y - 1)**2) Z = (Z1 - Z2) * 2 fig, axs = plt.subplots(2, 1) axs[0].contourf(X, Y, Z) CS = axs[1].contourf(X, Y, Z) labels = axs[1].clabel(CS) plt.show() ``` ### Actual outcome ### Expected outcome ### Additional information _No response_ ### Operating system _No response_ ### Matplotlib Version 0.2.0.dev53938+unknown.gd5f597d3d.g0f46f27f5 ### Matplotlib Backend _No response_ ### Python version _No response_ ### Jupyter version _No response_ ### Installation None
comments_text: I always assumed that `clabel` was only meant to be used for unfilled contours. However, now I look, I can't find any documentation to that effect. FWIW, I tried the example under v3.7.2 (`ContourSet` was re-designed at v3.8) and it was worse.

Since there appears to be no documented support for calling `clabel` on a filled `ContourSet`, would it be appropriate to emit a `_api.warn_external()` warning in the interim? Happy to open a PR for that if it seems like a useful step.
I think a warning sounds reasonable.  I'm relatively certain this would be almost impossible to fix and the appropriate way to label contours is to use line contours as @rcomer suggested.

comments_text: Hello @quzhijing thanks for the report.  Please could you provide a complete code example that we can run to reproduce the problem?

@rcomer  my complete code: 

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Set the plotting range
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))
plt.xlim(-10, 10)
plt.ylim(-10, 10)
plt.axhline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.5)
plt.axvline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.5)
plt.grid(color='gray', linestyle='--', linewidth=0.5)

# Define vectors
v1 = np.array([3, 4])
v2 = np.array([4, -3])

# Plot vectors v1 and v2
plt.quiver(0, 0, v1[0], v1[1], angles='xy', scale_units='xy', scale=1, color='r', label='v1 (3, 4)')
plt.quiver(0, 0, v2[0], v2[1], angles='xy', scale_units='xy', scale=1, color='b', label='v2 (4, -3)')

# Plot the sum of the vectors
v_sum = v1 + v2
line1 = plt.quiver(0, 0, v_sum[0], v_sum[1], angles='xy', scale_units='xy', scale=1, color='g', label='v1 + v2')
line1.set_linestyle('dashed')

# Plot the difference of the vectors
v_diff = v1 - v2
plt.quiver(0, 0, v_diff[0], v_diff[1], angles='xy', scale_units='xy', scale=1, color='y', linestyle='dashed',  facecolor='none', linewidth=1, label='v1 - v2')

# Plot scalar multiplication
scalar = 2
v_scalar = scalar * v1
plt.quiver(0, 0, v_scalar[0], v_scalar[1], angles='xy', scale_units='xy', scale=1, color='m', linestyle='dashed',  facecolor='none', linewidth=1, label='2 * v1')

# Plot vector dot product (projection explanation)
v2_unit = v2 / np.linalg.norm(v2)
projection_length = np.dot(v1, v2_unit)
projection = projection_length * v2_unit
plt.quiver(0, 0, projection[0], projection[1], angles='xy', scale_units='xy', scale=1, color='c', linestyle='dashed',  facecolor='none', linewidth=1, label='proj_v2(v1)')

# Add legend
plt.legend()

# Display the plot
plt.title('Basic Vector Operations in Linear Algebra')
plt.show()
```

A minimal reproducer:

```python
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))
line1 = plt.quiver(0, 0, 7, 1, angles='xy', scale_units='xy', scale=1, color='g', label='v1 + v2', lw=0)
line1.set_linestyle('dashed')
plt.legend()
fig = plt.gcf()
plt.show()
```

setting `lw` to be >0 or not showing the legend fixes the problem.  Something is going wrong in generating the legend entry, I think because it is grabbing a post-scaled version of the dash pattern.
author: quzhijing

title: `stairs` with dashed linestyle and fill=True raises ValueError
body:
If you change `test_stairs_options` to `mpl20` style, then it crashes. What we have worked out is that it is due to artist "G", which sets `ls='--', fill=True`, but does _not_ set a `linewidth`.

The setup for `stairs` sets a default `linewidth=0` if `fill=True`. The difference between classic and mpl20 styles is that the latter performs `linewidth`-scaling on the `linestyle`. So in mpl20 style, the dashes are scaled to 0, which raises in the backend.

>               raise ValueError(
>                     'At least one value in the dash list must be positive')
> E               ValueError: At least one value in the dash list must be positive

> lib/matplotlib/backend_bases.py:897: ValueError
> ``` 

 _Originally posted by @QuLogic in [#31300](https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/pull/31300/changes/d1d3d894a39e79b6d740a6586909714cd68fc3ef#r2932743766)_
comments_text: Could be something similar to #28298, where @rcomer had originally written #29302, but I think that was closed as it was leaking out into user code.

I opened a PR for this

I was able to reproduce the failure locally with:

```python
plt.style.use("mpl20")
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.stairs([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], fill=True, ls="--")
fig.canvas.draw()
```
The PR fixes this at draw time by avoiding application of dash patterns when the effective linewidth is zero, so it stays narrowly scoped to rendering and does not change stored linestyle state or getter behavior.

I think we’ve discussed in several places that our linestyle handling is kind of a mess, and properly sorting it out will be complicated.  For the current issue, it seems to me that if a user sets a linestyle then they want to see the line, so we should just not set the width to zero in that case.

title: [Bug]: AutoDateLocator confused by repeated hour at DST switch
body: ### Bug summary At the end of the daylight saving time period (or summer time in Europe), one hour is "repeated" in local time (`fold=1` in [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.fold)). AudoDateLocator doesn't handle this correctly, instead it scrambles the calculated numbers of ticks in the first half of the axis and leaves a gap in the second half in the following example: ### Code for reproduction ```Python from datetime import datetime from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo from matplotlib import pyplot as plt, dates as mdates tz = ZoneInfo("Europe/Berlin") x = [datetime(2026, 10, 25, 2, 0, tzinfo=tz), datetime(2026, 10, 25, 3, 0, tzinfo=tz)] fig, ax = plt.subplots() locator = mdates.AutoDateLocator(tz=tz) formatter = mdates.ConciseDateFormatter(locator, tz=tz) ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(locator) ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(formatter) ax.plot(x, [0, 1]) ``` ### Actual outcome ### Expected outcome evenly distributed ticks so that labels don't overlap also in the repeated section: ### Additional information _No response_ ### Operating system Windows ### Matplotlib Version 3.10.8 ### Matplotlib Backend any ### Python version 3.11.9 ### Jupyter version _No response_ ### Installation conda
comments_text: A related issue - which isn't necessarily a bug, but is nonetheless annoying - is described in https://discourse.matplotlib.org/t/autodatelocator-at-dst-time-switch/26180

Convert all tick datetimes to UTC before calling date2num inside RRuleLocator.tick_values().
This avoids DST fold (repeated hour) causing non-monotonic spacing. Then let the formatter display ticks back in the original timezone.

Someone would have to dive into this and see where the issue with rrule is and if it is even possible to repeat ticks. 

The idea of translating to UTC, deciding ticks, and then going back seems possible with some care.  

I would definitely say that folks should in general work in UTC so fixing this may be low on the priority list.

title: [Bug]: Changing limits by setting ticks does not emit "x/ylim_changed"
body: ### Bug summary "x/ylim_changed" should be emitted whenever the limits of an axis is changed. However, when the limits are changed via as a consequence of `ax.set_xticks`, the signal is not emitted. ### Code for reproduction ```Python import matplotlib.pyplot as plt def xlim_changed(ax): print(f'xlim changed to: {ax.get_xlim()}') fig, ax = plt.subplots() ax.callbacks.connect("xlim_changed", xlim_changed) ax.set_xlim(0.5, 1) # emits "xlim_changed" as expected ax.set_xticks([0, 100]) # does not emit "xlim_changed" ``` ### Actual outcome xlim changed to: (np.float64(0.5), np.float64(1.0)) ### Expected outcome xlim changed to: (np.float64(0.5), np.float64(1.0)) xlim changed to: (np.float64(0.0), np.float64(100.0)) ### Additional information I became aware of this bug while working on https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/pull/31214 for reference the plot produced by the above code looks like this: ### Operating system Ubuntu ### Matplotlib Version 3.11.0.dev ### Matplotlib Backend _No response_ ### Python version 3.13 ### Jupyter version _No response_ ### Installation git checkout

author: trygvrad

title: [Bug]: FigureCanvasTkAgg renders clipped/oversized when embedded in layout-managed container on Windows HiDPI
body:
### Bug summary

When `FigureCanvasTkAgg` is embedded in a layout-managed container (e.g., a `Frame` with `pack(fill=BOTH, expand=True)`), the plot is rendered larger than the visible canvas area on Windows with HiDPI display scaling (>100%). The right and bottom portions of the figure are clipped/cropped.

The root cause is in `FigureCanvasTk._update_device_pixel_ratio()`: it updates `figure.dpi` via `_set_device_pixel_ratio()` and then calls `self._tkcanvas.configure(width=physical_w, height=physical_h)`. When the canvas is constrained by a geometry manager (`pack`/`grid`), the actual displayed size does not change, so `<Configure>` does not fire, and `resize()` is never called to recalculate `figure.size_inches` with the new DPI. This results in a render buffer that is `device_pixel_ratio` times larger than the visible area.

### Code for reproduction

```Python
"""
Run on Windows with display scaling > 100%.
Expected: Plot fills the visible canvas area correctly.
Actual: Plot is rendered larger than visible area; right/bottom portions are clipped.
"""
import ctypes
import tkinter as tk
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.figure import Figure
from matplotlib.backends.backend_tkagg import FigureCanvasTkAgg

# Standard Windows HiDPI setup
ctypes.windll.shcore.SetProcessDpiAwareness(1)

root = tk.Tk()
root.geometry("800x600")

frame = tk.Frame(root)
frame.pack(fill=tk.BOTH, expand=True)

fig = Figure(dpi=96)
ax = fig.add_subplot(111)
x = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 100)
ax.plot(x, np.sin(x), label="sin(x)")
ax.plot(x, np.cos(x), label="cos(x)")
ax.set_xlabel("X Axis - may be clipped")
ax.set_ylabel("Y Axis - may be clipped")
ax.set_title("Embedded FigureCanvasTkAgg - HiDPI clipping bug")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True)
fig.tight_layout()

canvas = FigureCanvasTkAgg(fig, master=frame)
canvas.get_tk_widget().pack(fill=tk.BOTH, expand=True)

# Diagnostic output
def diagnostics(event=None):
    w = canvas.get_tk_widget()
    cfg_w, cfg_h = int(w["width"]), int(w["height"])
    act_w, act_h = w.winfo_width(), w.winfo_height()
    sz = fig.get_size_inches()
    render_w, render_h = int(sz[0] * fig.dpi), int(sz[1] * fig.dpi)
    print(f"device_pixel_ratio: {canvas.device_pixel_ratio}")
    print(f"figure.dpi: {fig.dpi} (original: {fig._original_dpi})")
    print(f"figure.get_size_inches(): [{sz[0]:.2f}, {sz[1]:.2f}]")
    print(f"Render size: {render_w}x{render_h}")
    print(f"Canvas configured: {cfg_w}x{cfg_h}")
    print(f"Canvas actual:     {act_w}x{act_h}")
    if render_w != act_w or render_h != act_h:
        print(f"BUG: render size ({render_w}x{render_h}) != "
              f"actual size ({act_w}x{act_h})")

root.after(500, diagnostics)
canvas.draw()
root.mainloop()
```

### Actual outcome

On Windows 11 with 150% display scaling:

```
device_pixel_ratio: 1.5
figure.dpi: 144.0 (original: 96)
figure.get_size_inches(): [8.33, 6.25]
Render size: 1200x900
Canvas configured: 1200x900
Canvas actual:     800x600
BUG: render size (1200x900) != actual size (800x600)
```

The plot is rendered at 1200×900 pixels but only 800×600 pixels are visible. The bottom and right portions (axis labels, legend, etc.) are cropped.

Note: this also reproduces **without** manually setting `tk scaling` — just `SetProcessDpiAwareness(1)` is sufficient, because Tk automatically adjusts its scaling factor on HiDPI displays, and `_update_device_pixel_ratio` reads it.

### Expected outcome

The plot should be fully visible within the canvas area with no clipping, regardless of the display scaling factor. The render size should match the actual displayed size.

### Additional information

The chain of events:

1. Canvas is packed → `<Configure>` fires with actual size (800×600) → `resize()` sets `figure.size_inches = (800/96, 600/96) = (8.33, 6.25)`
2. Canvas becomes visible → `<Map>` fires → `_update_device_pixel_ratio()` runs
3. `_set_device_pixel_ratio(1.5)` changes `figure.dpi` from 96 to 144
4. `configure(width=1200, height=900)` sets the canvas **requested** size
5. `pack(fill=BOTH, expand=True)` constrains the **actual** size to 800×600
6. Since actual size didn't change, `<Configure>` does NOT fire
7. `figure.size_inches` is still `(8.33, 6.25)` (calculated with the old dpi=96)
8. Render size = `size_inches × new_dpi = (8.33×144, 6.25×144) = (1200, 900)`
9. But only 800×600 is visible → **right and bottom portions are clipped**

This works correctly in `FigureManagerTk` (matplotlib's own window) because the canvas is packed directly in the top-level window, and `configure()` causes the window to resize, which triggers `<Configure>` → `resize()`. But when the canvas is embedded in a user-managed layout, the geometry manager constrains the size and `<Configure>` may not fire.

### Suggested fix 

After `_set_device_pixel_ratio` changes the DPI and `configure` sets the new requested size, `_update_device_pixel_ratio` should ensure `resize()` is called even if `<Configure>` doesn't fire. For example:

```python
def _update_device_pixel_ratio(

author: QuLogic

title: [MNT]: Inconsistency in log-scale handling: `bbox_inches='tight'` creates massive canvas for negative text coordinates
body:
### Summary

### Problem Description

When using `ax.set_yscale('log')`, Matplotlib correctly ignores/masks negative values in data series, and also Artists like text placed at a negative coordinate. However, when combined with `savefig(..., bbox_inches='tight')`, the layout engine attempts to include these "invisible" texts, resulting in a figure with a massive white canvas and a microscopic plot.



### Minimal Reproducible Example

### 1. The Normal Case (Data Masking)

This script shows that Matplotlib handles negative **data** gracefully. It simply doesn't draw the parts of the line that go below zero on a log scale.

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data goes from -10 to 10
x = np.linspace(0, 10, 100)
y = np.sin(x) * 10 

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(x, y, label='Sine Wave')
ax.set_yscale('log')
ax.set_title("1. Data with negatives is masked correctly")

plt.savefig("1_normal_data.png")


```

---

### 2. The "Hidden Danger" (Negative Text)

Here, we add text at negative places. Everything looks fine because the window is usually sized to the **axes**, not the **artists**. The text is simply "off-screen" in a mathematical void.

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

x = np.linspace(0, 10, 100)
y = np.sin(x) * 10 

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(x, y)
ax.set_yscale('log')

# Adding text at a negative Y-coordinate
ax.text(5, -5, "I am at y = -5", color='red', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_title("2. Text at y=-5 (Looks okay in viewer)")

plt.savefig("2_text_no_tight.png")


```

---

### 3. The "Explosion" (`bbox_inches='tight'`)

This is the script that triggers the issue. By using `bbox_inches='tight'`, you tell Matplotlib to "shrink-wrap" the image around **every** artist. Because it results in an extreme coordinate, the "shrink-wrap" stretches across thousands of virtual pixels.

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

x = np.linspace(0, 10, 100)
y = np.sin(x) * 10 

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(x, y)
ax.set_yscale('log')

# This text coordinate will 'hallucinate' a massive distance in log-space
ax.text(5, -5, "I am at y = -5", color='red')
ax.set_title("3. The Explosion (Check the saved file!)")

# This specific command triggers the canvas stretching
plt.savefig("3_canvas_explosion.png", bbox_inches='tight')


```

### Actual Behavior

The log transform of a negative number (e.g., ) results in a non-finite or extreme proxy value (maybe). The `tight_layout` / `bbox_inches` logic includes this coordinate in the bounding box calculation, stretching the figure canvas to extreme proportions.

### Expected Behavior

Ideally, Artists with mathematically undefined coordinates on the current scale should be treated similarly to data points:

1. They should be excluded from the bounding box calculation for `bbox_inches='tight'`.
2. Or, at least they should trigger a warning that an Artist is located at an undefined coordinate for the chosen scale, which may result in an unexpected figure.

### Suggested Mitigation

I know matplotlib provides tools like set_in_layout to control whether an artist is to be included in layout calculations, but `bbox_inches='tight'` originally tries to figure out the tight bbox of the figure [says the documentation], so I would suggest not considering those items when deciding bbox, or at least raise some warning in the early versions.



### Proposed fix

For the modification, I would recommend adding some filters in [get_tightbbox of artist.py](https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/blob/1392cbe3c79cdb93f9282747841d648770f60249/lib/matplotlib/artist.py#L348-L373) for situations of scale=='log'.

[A part of expressions is refined by GPT but manually checked]
author: leakyH

title: [Bug]: `ax.hist()` fails on sequence of timedeltas due to comparison with `np.inf`
body:
### Bug summary

I expected `ax.hist([sequence of timedeltas])` to Just Work, as it does with `ax.hist([sequence of timestamps])`. However, there is a comparison with `np.inf` that works when the sequence is of timestamps and fails when the sequence is of timedeltas; numpy cannot promote a `TimeDelta64DType` to a float: https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/blob/main/lib/matplotlib/axes/_axes.py#L7479

### Code for reproduction

```Python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10, 7))
ax = fig.add_subplot(1, 1, 1)
ax.hist(pd.date_range("2026-01-01", "2026-01-05", freq="D"))
ax.hist(pd.date_range("2026-01-01", "2026-01-05", freq="D").diff(1))
```

### Actual outcome

```
numpy.exceptions.DTypePromotionError: The DType <class 'numpy.dtypes.TimeDelta64DType'> could not be promoted by <class 'numpy.dtypes._PyFloatDType'>. This means that no common DType exists for the given inputs. For example they cannot be stored in a single array unless the dtype is `object`. The full list of DTypes is: (<class 'numpy.dtypes.TimeDelta64DType'>, <class 'numpy.dtypes._PyFloatDType'>)

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<python-input-5>", line 1, in <module>
    ax.hist(pd.date_range("2026-01-01", "2026-01-05", freq="D").diff(1))
    ~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/kylepenner/Documents/numpy/lib/python3.13/site-packages/matplotlib/_api/deprecation.py", line 477, in wrapper
    return func(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/Users/kylepenner/Documents/numpy/lib/python3.13/site-packages/matplotlib/__init__.py", line 1497, in inner
    return func(
        ax,
        *map(cbook.sanitize_sequence, args),
        **{k: cbook.sanitize_sequence(v) for k, v in kwargs.items()})
  File "/Users/kylepenner/Documents/numpy/lib/python3.13/site-packages/matplotlib/axes/_axes.py", line 7479, in hist
    xmin = min(xmin, np.nanmin(xi))
numpy._core._exceptions._UFuncNoLoopError: ufunc 'less' did not contain a loop with signature matching types (<class 'numpy.dtypes.TimeDelta64DType'>, <class 'numpy.dtypes._PyFloatDType'>) -> None
```


### Expected outcome

There's an easy way around this---convert the timedeltas to floats with `.total_seconds()`---but I do prefer the `hist` call to just work.

### Additional information

_No response_

### Operating system

_No response_

3.11.0.dev1799+g6dbc0c7cd

_No response_

_No response_

git checkout
comments_text:
Hii
I reproduce this issue on current main.

 ### Environment:
* Python 3.12
* Linux (Ubuntu)
* Matplotlib built from source

The failure occurs during histogram range computation in `Axes.hist `, where `xmin = np.inf` and `xmax = -np.inf` are initialized and later compared against `numpy.timedelta64`. After `_process_unit_info` and `convert_xunits`, the data still contains `timedelta64`, so the min/max logic involving `np.inf` triggers the `DTypePromotionError`.

I'm looking into adjusting the range computataion to avoid comparisons with `np.inf` for non-numeric dtypes.
@joddeepesh-cloud    **Yes ,** I'm actively working on a fix and looking into improving the range computation for non-numeric dtypes like `timedelta64`. I'll update with a proposed fix.

title: BUG: RectangleSelector modifies view
body:
<!--To help us understand and resolve your issue, please fill out the form to the best of your ability.-->
<!--You can feel free to delete the sections that do not apply.-->

### Bug report

**Bug summary**

RectangleSelector:

1. Modifies `dataLim`
2. This modification seemingly cannot be reverted by removing the patch, or by modifying the patch properties

Basically I want to be able to use RectangleSelector without it modifying `ax.dataLim`, because if it does it messes up `autoscale`. 

**Code for reproduction**

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.patches import Rectangle
from matplotlib.widgets import RectangleSelector

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
lim = ax.dataLim
ax.plot([-0.1, 0.1], [-0.1, 0.1])
assert lim.x0 == lim.y0 == -0.1
assert lim.width == lim.height == 0.2
for kind in ('rect', 'rs'):
    if kind == 'rect':
        rect = Rectangle((0, 0), 0, 1)
        ax.add_patch(rect)
    else:
        rs = RectangleSelector(ax, lambda x: None)
        rect = rs.to_draw
    assert isinstance(rect, Rectangle)
    ax.relim()
    assert lim.x0 == lim.y0 == -0.1
    assert lim.width == 0.2
    assert lim.height == 1.1
    ax.patches.remove(rect)
    assert ax.patches == []
    ax.relim()
    assert lim.height == 0.2, (kind, lim.height)
```
The plain "Rectangle" version passes, but the "RectangleSelector" version does not:
```
    assert lim.height == 0.2, (kind, lim.height)
AssertionError: ('rs', 1.1)
```
And the resulting plot is "stuck" with a minimum autolim y max of 1. I also tried setting properties of the Rectangle patch that's part of the RectangleSelector (xy, width, height) and calling `ax.relim()` but the bound of `1.` never decreases.

**Matplotlib version**

<!--Please specify your platform and versions of the relevant libraries you are using:-->
  * Operating system: Linux x86_64
  * Matplotlib version: latest `master`
  * Matplotlib backend: Qt5Agg
  * Python version: 3.8

body: ### Bug summary When I create 2x1 subplots with constrained layout, minor adjustment of the axes' position causes its size to decrease. ### Code for reproduction ```Python import matplotlib.pyplot as plt from matplotlib.patches import Rectangle fig, (ax0, ax1) = plt.subplots(2, 1, layout='constrained') ax0.add_patch(Rectangle((0, 0), 2, 2, hatch='/o', fill=False)) ax1.add_patch(Rectangle((0, 0), 2, 2, hatch='\\|', fill=False)) l, b, w, h = ax0.get_position().bounds delta = 0.01 ax0.set_position([l, b+delta, w, h]) plt.show() ``` ### Actual outcome ### Expected outcome ### Additional information _No response_ ### Operating system _No response_ ### Matplotlib Version 0.2.0.dev53776+unknown.g607d2c380.ge3d6dfc70 ### Matplotlib Backend _No response_ ### Python version _No response_ ### Jupyter version _No response_ ### Installation None
comments_text:
This has the same answer to your previous issue

Ax.set_position turns layout management off for ax.  

The position of ax is not set until the figure is drawn for the first time.  If you'd like to trigger a draw then fig.draw_without_rendering(). 

But in general, constrained_layout and set_position are explicitly not compatible.

title: [Bug]: Large pixels may overlap when using imshow()
body: ### Bug summary When using `imshow()` with a small array (large pixels), the pixel patches can end up slightly misaligned. This becomes especially obvious when thin grid lines are overlaid on the figure. ### Code for reproduction ```Python import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import numpy as np fig,ax = plt.subplots() N = 12 ax.imshow(np.random.rand(N, N)) seps = np.arange(-0.5, N) ax.vlines(seps, -0.5, N - 0.5, linewidth=1) ax.hlines(seps, -0.5, N - 0.5, linewidth=1) plt.show() ``` ### Actual outcome ### Expected outcome A figure where none of the pixel patches overlap their respective grid lines. ### Additional information I originally thought this was the same issue as #14143, see [my original comment](https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/14143#issuecomment-584833923). Eventually that bug was fixed but this issue remained. [To quote @ayshih](https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/14143#issuecomment-3781269196): > Based on my investigation so far, the problem in your example is indeed distinct from the original issue, and it is very important to fix. It's not a bug internal to the image-resampling code, but rather it has to do with how image placement affects what resampling is requested and how the resampled image is drawn. ### Operating system Debian ### Matplotlib Version 3.11.0.dev1652+gddd4196c7 (but first found around 3.3.0) ### Matplotlib Backend _No response_ ### Python version _No response_ ### Jupyter version _No response_ ### Installation uv

title: [Bug]: 3D Lines can't be drawn over patches with `computed_zorder`
body:
### Bug summary

I am very happy with the `computed_zorder` for 3D projection in general, but this block of code (from https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/blob/main/lib/mpl_toolkits/mplot3d/axes3d.py) prevents my lines (or collections) from being drawn over a set of disks in a 3D plot

```
        collections_and_patches = (
            artist for artist in self._children
            if isinstance(artist, (mcoll.Collection, mpatches.Patch))
            and artist.get_visible())
        if self.computed_zorder:
            # Calculate projection of collections and patches and zorder
            # them. Make sure they are drawn above the grids.
            zorder_offset = max(axis.get_zorder()
                                for axis in self._axis_map.values()) + 1
            collection_zorder = patch_zorder = zorder_offset

            for artist in sorted(collections_and_patches,
                                 key=lambda artist: artist.do_3d_projection(),
                                 reverse=True):
                if isinstance(artist, mcoll.Collection):
                    artist.zorder = collection_zorder
                    collection_zorder += 1
                elif isinstance(artist, mpatches.Patch):
                    artist.zorder = patch_zorder
                    patch_zorder += 1
```

I don't want to have to manually set the Z-order, since the rotation works and the code is 90% functional, but the fact that the `zorder` of patches and collections are set independently breaks my ability to layer lines over disks. I am using this for a "flat shaded" style geometry. Can this restriction be loosened?



### Code for reproduction

```Python
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection='3d') 
ax.plot([0, .5], [0, .5], [0, .5])
ax.scatter([.5], [.5], [.5], s=100, color='red')
ax.plot([.5, 1], [.5, 2], [.5, 1])

https://matplotlib.org/devdocs/api/_as_gen/matplotlib.quiver.Quiver.html#matplotlib.quiver.Quiver

### Problem

The documentation states that "mid" can be used as a synonym for "middle" however this is not implemented in the code: 

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "c:\Users\mean\OneDrive - COWI\Documents\04_code\triaxial_concrete_complementary_energy\example.py", line 147, in <module>
    plot_stress_axes(mat, ax, ttl)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "c:\Users\mean\OneDrive - COWI\Documents\04_code\triaxial_concrete_complementary_energy\example.py", line 120, in plot_stress_axes
    ax.quiver(
    ~~~~~~~~~^
        0,
        ^^
    ...<8 lines>...
        pivot="mid",
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^
    )
    ^
  File "C:\code\python_venvs\triax_concrete\Lib\site-packages\matplotlib\__init__.py", line 1524, in inner
    return func(
        ax,
        *map(cbook.sanitize_sequence, args),
        **{k: cbook.sanitize_sequence(v) for k, v in kwargs.items()})
  File "C:\code\python_venvs\triax_concrete\Lib\site-packages\mpl_toolkits\mplot3d\axes3d.py", line 3298, in quiver
    _api.check_in_list(['tail', 'middle', 'tip'], pivot=pivot)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "C:\code\python_venvs\triax_concrete\Lib\site-packages\matplotlib\_api\__init__.py", line 144, in check_in_list
    raise ValueError(msg)
ValueError: 'mid' is not a valid value for pivot; supported values are 'tail', 'middle', 'tip'
```

### Suggested improvement

Remove "mid" as an option in the docs

Before:
```python
pivot : {'tail', 'mid', 'middle', 'tip'}, default: 'tail'
    The part of the arrow that is anchored to the *X*, *Y* grid. The arrow
    rotates about this point.

    'mid' is a synonym for 'middle'.
```

```python
pivot : {'tail', 'middle', 'tip'}, default: 'tail'
    The part of the arrow that is anchored to the *X*, *Y* grid. The arrow
    rotates about this point.
```

author: MEMAndersen

title: [Bug]: Negative figure size in QSplitter.
body:
### Bug summary

I use matplotlib + PyQt5. The figure is placed inside a QSplitter (a standard Qt widget that consists of several vertical or horizontal parts which the handles between those parts can resize). When a part of QSplitter shrinks to a zero size, it automatically resizes the widget it contains. I already had an issue with this (#17093), but now I have a new one. 

When there are other widgets in the same splitter part with a figure, the figure becomes negatively sized.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1112285/228280777-563a4be3-0461-4e30-a216-bf4e0ef565bf.png)
Here, a splitter consists of two parts: the top one with one button and the bottom one with a figure and a button in a vertical layout. When I pull the split line all the way down, I get an exception that the figure has a negative size.

### Code for reproduction

```python
import sys

from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QMainWindow, QApplication, QVBoxLayout

from matplotlib.backends.backend_qt5agg import FigureCanvasQTAgg as FigureCanvas
from matplotlib.figure import Figure

from PyQt5 import QtCore, QtGui, QtWidgets


class Ui_MainWindow(object):
    def setupUi(self, MainWindow):
        MainWindow.setObjectName("MainWindow")
        MainWindow.resize(800, 600)
        self.centralwidget = QtWidgets.QWidget(MainWindow)
        self.centralwidget.setObjectName("centralwidget")
        self.gridLayout = QtWidgets.QGridLayout(self.centralwidget)
        self.gridLayout.setObjectName("gridLayout")
        self.splitter = QtWidgets.QSplitter(self.centralwidget)
        self.splitter.setOrientation(QtCore.Qt.Vertical)
        self.splitter.setObjectName("splitter")
        self.pushButton = QtWidgets.QPushButton(self.splitter)
        self.pushButton.setObjectName("pushButton")
        self.widget = QtWidgets.QWidget(self.splitter)
        self.widget.setObjectName("widget")
        self.verticalLayout = QtWidgets.QVBoxLayout(self.widget)
        self.verticalLayout.setContentsMargins(0, 0, 0, 0)
        self.verticalLayout.setObjectName("verticalLayout")
        self.plot = QtWidgets.QWidget(self.widget)
        self.plot.setObjectName("plot")
        self.verticalLayout.addWidget(self.plot)
        self.pushButton_2 = QtWidgets.QPushButton(self.widget)
        self.pushButton_2.setObjectName("pushButton_2")
        self.verticalLayout.addWidget(self.pushButton_2)
        self.gridLayout.addWidget(self.splitter, 0, 0, 1, 1)
        MainWindow.setCentralWidget(self.centralwidget)
        self.menubar = QtWidgets.QMenuBar(MainWindow)
        self.menubar.setGeometry(QtCore.QRect(0, 0, 800, 32))
        self.menubar.setObjectName("menubar")
        MainWindow.setMenuBar(self.menubar)
        self.statusbar = QtWidgets.QStatusBar(MainWindow)
        self.statusbar.setObjectName("statusbar")
        MainWindow.setStatusBar(self.statusbar)

        self.retranslateUi(MainWindow)
        QtCore.QMetaObject.connectSlotsByName(MainWindow)

    def retranslateUi(self, MainWindow):
        _translate = QtCore.QCoreApplication.translate
        MainWindow.setWindowTitle(_translate("MainWindow", "MainWindow"))
        self.pushButton.setText(_translate("MainWindow", "PUSH"))
        self.pushButton_2.setText(_translate("MainWindow", "PUSH2"))

class MainWindow(QMainWindow):
    def __init__(self, *args):
        super().__init__(*args)

        self.ui = Ui_MainWindow()
        self.ui.setupUi(self)

        self.figure = Figure()
        self.canvas = FigureCanvas(self.figure)

        layout = QVBoxLayout()
        layout.addWidget(self.canvas)
        self.ui.plot.setLayout(layout)

app = QApplication(sys.argv)

mw = MainWindow()
mw.show()

app.exec()
```


### Actual outcome

ValueError: figure size must be positive finite not [ 7.76 -0.06]

### Expected outcome

Not seeing an exception.

### Additional information

_No response_

### Operating system

Kubuntu 22.04

### Matplotlib Version

3.7.1

### Matplotlib Backend

QtAgg

### Python version

Python 3.10.6

### Jupyter version

_No response_

### Installation

pip
comments_text:
https://doc.qt.io/qtforpython/PySide6/QtWidgets/QWidget.html#PySide6.QtWidgets.PySide6.QtWidgets.QWidget.setMinimumHeight

I would recommend something like setting the minimum height(/width) in Qt to prevent the case where the error can occur.

A negative size is a non-sensical thing that _should_ likely error from mpl's perspective. (Even 0 is arguable at least).

Setting a minimum may prevent you from completely collapsing the splitter (though I actually think in my experience you can still... haven't tested it recently though)

The alternative would be to catch the resize signal and process it/ignore the error.

If setting a negative size is a Qt convention for saying "on screen but not visible/squashed) I think putting a cut-out in the Qt backend to clip this to 0 and/or short-circuit draw as a no-op would make sense.

Setting the minimum height of the widget that contains a figure and a button to some small but not too small value helps. Splitter still collapses completely, but the error is no more. 

Yes, clipping to 0 would be a nice thing to have because QSplitters are weird.
author: Abbath

title: [Bug]: figure displays off-screen when figsize is specified with multiple monitors
body:
### Bug summary

When `figsize` is specified in `figure()`, the render displays partially offscreen and on the wrong monitor if the process was spawned on a secondary monitor.

### Code for reproduction

```Python
import numpy as np
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt

x_in = np.linspace(-1, 1, 320)
y_in = np.linspace(-1, 1, 320)

plt.figure("bug example", figsize=(10, 8))
plt.scatter(x_in, y_in, color="red", marker=".", s=0.5)
plt.gca().set_aspect("equal")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```


### Actual outcome

When the process is executed from a secondary monitor, the plot displays partially offscreen on the system primary monitor and the control bar is not accessible until the window is resized.

![image](https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/assets/6363721/c6d9e53c-c9ea-4c03-8929-c4cd0aeb175e)

### Expected outcome

I expect the window to open on the monitor that spawns the process, and be centered on the screen.

After removing `figsize=(10,8)`:
![image](https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/assets/6363721/c07f74a7-5b23-4177-abd5-72ea2bb7eba5)


### Additional information

My secondary monitor is a different resolution than my main monitor. I do not have another monitor with the same resolution to test this with.

### Operating system

Windows 11

### Matplotlib Version

3.9.0

### Matplotlib Backend

qtagg

### Python version

3.12.3

### Jupyter version

_No response_

### Installation

conda
comments_text:
Is the call to `plt.tight_layout` required to cause this?

Does the problem depend on the value of `figsize` (notable relative to the actual size of your screen)?

I should have tested that first point...`plt.tight_layout` is not required to cause this. It seems to depend entirely on the `figsize` parameter. Values close to, but still smaller than, the size of the secondary monitor cause it to display offscreen on the primary monitor which has a larger resolution.
Closing as inactive. Please report back if the issue continues to exist.

title: Yale Access Bluetooth Stuck on Initializing/Failed Setup with newer ESP-IDF
body: ### The problem **Lock:** Yale Assure Lock 2 Key-Free Touchscreen with Bluetooth **Bluetooth Adapter:** Olimex-esp32-poe-iso-fa6d38 **ESPHome Firmware:** 26.1.5 (ESPHome 2026.1.5) **Problem:** So I've been using this integration for years and never run into _this_ issue before. Immediately after updating to **Home Assistant OS 17.1** on the morning of Feb 13th I found that the Yale Access Bluetooth integration was failing to fully initialize and throwing this error: > Failed setup, will retry: No advertisement received before timeout; Try moving the Bluetooth adapter closer to LF002C2Failed setup, will retry: No advertisement received before timeout; Try moving the Bluetooth adapter closer to LF002C2 What's strange about this is my Bluetooth coverage has not changed and the Lock has never had issues with being outside of the Bluetooth range. (Also when I remove the Yale Access Bluetooth integration the Lock is found automatically again indicating it is indeed within range). Also **while connecting** the Bluetooth adapters section shows the Lock as the one device as connected: That being said when the integration finally times out it no longer shows connected: I have only two other Bluetooth devices: iBeacon monitoring of one device and one BThome motion sensor device that are still working via Bluetooth as normal (and don't use a connection slot) so I think the adapter is fine. As it seemed like the update to Home Assistant OS 17.1 messed up the offline key I ran through the process to rotate and obtain a new own as outlined in the Integrations setup instructions but the issue persisted. During this time Home Assistant core-2026.2.2 came out so I tried updating to that to see if perhaps it would resolve the issue but it persisted. As a quick fix I restored to a core 2026.2.1 backup that used Home Assistant OS 17.0...only to realize _the OS_ isn't backed up/restored so I'm still on 17.1. I didn't think to reload a Snapshot on my VM so I thought I'd mention this issue here since I'm in too deep now. What is strange is that during the backup restores, offline key rotations and Integration reloads the Integration never seemed to "ask me" for and updated Offline key which I believe it used to do if I rotated the Offline Key with the owner account in the Yale Access Android App. Is anyone else having this issue? I'm wondering if it was just bad timing for me and more will have this issue on 17.1 once the time comes for them to re-auth their offline key for any reason. **Troubleshoot Steps:** - Rebooted Home Assistant - Power cycled Bluetooth adapter - Replaced Batteries in Lock - Locked/Unlocked and Recalibrated Lock with Yale Android App using Owner account to attempt to force rotation and storage of new Offline Key - Disabled/Re-enabled Yale Access Bluetooth Integration while keeping [August (hub) Integration](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/august) active; never prompted for Offline Key - Disabled/Re-enabled both Yale Access Bluetooth Integration and August (hub) Integration; never prompted for Offline Key - Removed/Re-installed both Yale Access Bluetooth Integration and August (hub) Integration (was prompted to login and use MFA from August Integration; never prompted for Offline Key - Tried above troubleshooting steps with both Home Assistant core-2026.2.2 and Home Assistant core-2026.2.1 ### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue? core-2026.2.1; core-2026.2.2 ### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core? core-2026.2.1 ### What type of installation are you running? Home Assistant OS ### Integration causing the issue Yale Access Bluetooth ### Link to integration documentation on our website https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/yalexs_ble/ ### Diagnostics information I found some errors mentioning Yale in the logs but did **not find any logs mentioning August.** ### Example YAML snippet ```yaml ``` ### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us? ```txt 2026-02-14 14:09:27.183 ERROR (MainThread) [yalexs_ble.push] FRONT DOOR (LF002C2): Disconnected while updating Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/yalexs_ble/session.py", line 269, in execute return await self._write(command, command_name) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/yalexs_ble/session.py", line 139, in _write return await self._locked_write(command, command_name) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-pac

title: Roborock re-auth loop: RoborockException: parameter error - response code: 1002 on Core 2026.3.0 (Python 3.14)
body:
### The problem

After updating to Core 2026.3.0, the Roborock integration is stuck in a persistent re-authentication loop. While the verification code is received and accepted by the UI, the backend fails to finalize the token exchange, resulting in a "parameter error - response code: 1002".
Environment
Home Assistant Core: 2026.3.0
Installation Type: Home Assistant OS 17.1
Python Version: 3.14 (bundled with 2026.3.x)
Integration: Roborock (Core)
Steps to Reproduce
Trigger the "Re-authenticate" repair flow.
Submit the email and enter the received verification code.
The UI displays a generic error or the "Authentication expired" notification returns within minutes.
Logs show the 1002 exception immediately following the code entry.
Logs:

2026-03-05 09:01:XX ERROR (MainThread) [homeassistant.components.roborock.config_flow] Unexpected exception
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/components/roborock/config_flow.py", line 114, in async_step_code
    user_data = await self._client.code_login_v4(code)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.14/site-packages/roborock/web_api.py", line 348, in code_login_v4
    raise RoborockException(f"{login_response.get('msg')} - response code: {response_code}")
roborock.exceptions.RoborockException: parameter error - response code: 1002

(Note: Log excerpt confirms the failure occurs during the code_login_v4 function call.)
Additional Information
The Roborock mobile app functions normally; this appears to be an API/handshake issue specific to the integration on Python 3.14.
Similar issues have been reported in previous version jumps where the underlying roborock library required updates for new API requirements.
I have verified that there are no pending EULAs or User Agreements to sign in the mobile app.

### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

2026.3.0

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

2026.2.3

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

Roborock

### Link to integration documentation on our website

_No response_

### Diagnostics information

_No response_

```yaml

```

### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?

```txt

```

### Additional information

_No response_

I should mention that it was also happening on 2026.2.3, and for about the past week, just as reported by AlbibiG. I think my issue is an exact mirror of his.

body:
### The problem

Hi, 

I'm running Docker version 20.10.3, build 55f0773 on Synology NAS (DSM 7.1.1-42962) and I was  using below commands to do a fresh install of HomeAssistant Supervisor each time a new version was released. 

docker stop hassio_observer hassio_multicast hassio_audio homeassistant hassio_dns hassio_cli hassio_supervisor
docker rm hassio_observer hassio_multicast hassio_audio homeassistant hassio_dns hassio_cli hassio_supervisor
docker rmi `docker images | grep amd64-hassio-audio | gawk '{ print $3 }'`
docker rmi `docker images | grep amd64-hassio-cli | gawk '{ print $3 }'`
docker rmi `docker images | grep amd64-hassio-dns | gawk '{ print $3 }'`
docker rmi `docker images | grep amd64-hassio-multicast | gawk '{ print $3 }'`
docker rmi `docker images | grep qemux86-64-homeassistant | gawk '{ print $3 }'`
docker rmi `docker images | grep amd64-hassio-observer | gawk '{ print $3 }'`
docker rmi `docker images | grep amd64-hassio-supervisor | gawk '{ print $3}'`

docker run -d 	--name=hassio_supervisor \
	-v /var/run/dbus:/var/run/dbus \
	-v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
	-v /volume1/docker/homeassistant:/data \
	-v /volume1/docker/homeassistant:/config \
	-e SUPERVISOR_SHARE=/volume1/docker/homeassistant \
	-e SUPERVISOR_NAME=hassio_supervisor \
	-e HOMEASSISTANT_REPOSITORY=homeassistant/qemux86-64-homeassistant \
	--security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
	--privileged  \
	--restart always \
	--device=/dev/ttyACM0 ghcr.io/home-assistant/amd64-hassio-supervisor:2026.02.3

Today I've attempted to do fresh install of 2026.02.3 version of Supervisor and I was presented with following error: 

===========================================================

Networking issue detected
Home Assistant OS detected a networking issue in your setup. As part of the initial setup, Home Assistant OS downloads the latest version of Home Assistant Core. This networking issue prevents this download. The network issue might be DNS related. The currently used DNS service is: ?.

To resolve this, you can try a different DNS server. Select one of the options below. Alternatively, change your router configuration to use your own custom DNS server.

Using above error I found similar issue https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/issues/24347 where users are saying that one of the workaround is to downgrade Docker version (as previous version is not using DHI - Docker Hardened Images).

title: Envoy reporting wrong energy lifetime from inverters when api could not be reached after startup
body:
### The problem

after a nightly restart of my home assistant the solar energy reported by the envoy integration was off and the energy dashboard shows a -146kWh drop.
a restart did not fix it.
looking at the lifetime energy of each inverter (all between 450-700 kWh) in HA, they do not match with the lifetime energy shown in the envoy app.
the logging states:
2026-03-11 05:24:42.334 ERROR (MainThread) [homeassistant.components.enphase_envoy.coordinator] Error fetching Envoy 122323003316 data: Error communicating with Envoy API on 192.168.0.217: HTTP status error https://192.168.0.217/ivp/pdm/device_data 502[0m

annoyingly the integration or the energy dashboard seem to have registered for today the life time energy as it was at the start of the previous month (i.e. 1 feb).
To me this seems very wrong, can't this be changed (thus not register false values)?

comments_text:
Hi @HomeAssistantPim, from your description I understand that after an envoy outage you see multiple issues:

- the energy dashboard shows a -146kWh drop.
- the energy dashboard seem to have registered for today the life time energy as it was at the start of the previous month (i.e. 1 feb).
- the lifetime energy of each inverter (all between 450-700 kWh) in HA, they do not match with the lifetime energy shown in the envoy app.

As for the energy dashboard, what entities are you using there. Is it the individual inverter lifetime values or the envoy lifetime value? Or is it the Envoy today's value?

As the energy dashboard runs of the statistics, you may be aware you can correct it by correcting statistics. Yes this is patching the effect, not the cause, but it will at least clean the data display.

As for the inverter lifetime values not matching the Enphase App. Did they match before this outage? The data is provided as received from the Envoy. If the values did match before and now no longer, there would be a change in values supplied by the Envoy.

Can you provide me with the HA Enphase integration [diagnostics report](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/diagnostics) and some more lines from the home-assistant log file covering the outage?

Furthermore can you share some HA history values (screenshot or csv export)  for (some) inverter and the envoy lifetime values over the outage, some 48 hours or so so I have a better understanding of the changes you are seeing?
@mib1185, any idea why this one never triggered the email to the integration owners?

[config_entry-enphase_envoy-c11aefcc5664272731efce4ce6982659.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26037236/config_entry-enphase_envoy-c11aefcc5664272731efce4ce6982659.json) @catsmanac Hi Arie, Please see the history of all the lifetime energy sensors. Oddly the history of the microinverters got cleared or recreated?

author: newtrim

title: Backup upload to Nabu Casa fails in 2026.4.0: Indicated size does not match actual size (10,240 byte discrepancy)
body:
### The problem

Since updating to Home Assistant 2026.4.0, uploading backups to Nabu Casa consistently fails. The logs report that the indicated size of the upload does not match the actual size. In every failure instance, the actual size is exactly 10,240 bytes larger than the indicated size.

**The Workaround**: Enabling encryption for local backups (so they match the Nabu Casa agent encryption setting) prevents the issue.

**Reproduction**: This bug is triggered by a mismatch in encryption settings between local backups and Nabu Casa:

- Local backups: unencrypted
- Nabu Casa agent: encryption required

When these settings match, the on-the-fly streamer is bypassed entirely and uploads succeed. This explains why most users are unaffected - I have not found any other bug report for this.

### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2026.4.0

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

core-2026.3.x

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant Container

### Integration causing the issue

Backup / Cloud

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/backup/

### Diagnostics information

N/A

### Example YAML snippet

```yaml
N/A
```

### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?

```txt
Logger: homeassistant.components.backup
Source: components/backup/manager.py:655
integration: Backup (documentation, issues)
First occurred: 3 April 2026 at 20:27:13 (4 occurrences)
Last logged: 05:42:48
Upload failed for cloud.cloud: Indicated size 1887559680 does not match actual size 1887569920
Upload failed for cloud.cloud: Indicated size 1888593920 does not match actual size 1888604160
Upload failed for cloud.cloud: Indicated size 1889157120 does not match actual size 1889167360
Upload failed for cloud.cloud: Indicated size 1803192320 does not match actual size 1803202560
```

### Additional information

Size difference calculations from the log output:
- 1887569920 - 1887559680 = 10,240 bytes
- 1888604160 - 1888593920 = 10,240 bytes
- 1889167360 - 1889157120 = 10,240 bytes
- 1803202560 - 1803192320 = 10,240 bytes

<details>
  <summary>Incorrect LLM analysis</summary>
**Analysis after reviewing securetar source** (Assisted by an LLM - should be verified before acting on it)
The root cause appears to be that `_CipherBackupStreamer.size()` in `util.py` calculates the declared upload size starting from `backup.size`, which is derived from `stat()` on the unencrypted file on disk. When `EncryptedBackupStreamer` encrypts the backup on the fly via `import_tar`, the output is larger than the input due to the securetar header overhead per inner tar file — this expansion is not accounted for in the size estimate. The `_num_tar_files() * tarfile.RECORDSIZE` padding added by `size()` does not fully compensate for this, leaving the declared `Content-Length` short by exactly one RECORDSIZE (10,240 bytes) in a standard Core/Container backup with no addons or folders.
Specifically, in `SecureTarArchive.import_tar()`, Python's tarfile `addfile` writes block-alignment padding after the encrypted content that is not included in `encrypted.ciphertext_size`. This extra padding is what causes the actual streamed bytes to exceed the declared size.
Note that this spans two repositories: The size calculation is in` _CipherBackupStreamer.size()` in `homeassistant-core/util.py`, but the padding behaviour that causes the discrepancy is in `import_tar()` in the securetar library.
</details>

**System Information**
OS: Asahi Linux (6.17.9-400.asahi.fc43.aarch64+16k)
CPU Arch: aarch64
Python: 3.14.2
Docker: True
comments_text: Same issue since 2026.4, glad it's not just me

The problem is that the implementation assumes the overhead added by the encryption is no more than a  fixed 10240 bytes per encrypted inner tar file. When uploading to NabuCasa, we thus reserve an additional 10240 bytes per inner tar file.

With securetar v3, there's _both_ a fixed size header, _and_ an additional 17 bytes / MB.

The backup in the example is ~1.8GB, and the reserved space is no longer enough.

Hi all, I wasn't sure if I actively had to follow up, but I did search through existing issues following the bot's instructions and it looks like at least 2 other times this was reported, but was either [_Closed_ ](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues/160461)or _[Closed as not planned](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues/102108)_. In both issues, users are reporting their devices are configured as IPv6, even though they don't actively use IPv6 in their setups. In all cases, their addresses are not in square brackets as mib1185 mentioned above.

Hopefully this is some helpful information.
author: MillerWDJr

title: Light toggle action no longer accepts color_temp since 2026.03
body:
### The problem

**Description**

Since updating to Home Assistant 2026.03 several of my automations stopped working. The issue appears to be related to the light.toggle action.

Previously I used brightness_pct and color_temp together with the toggle action. After the update this no longer works and Home Assistant throws an error saying that extra keys are not allowed.

It looks like the service validation has become stricter and these parameters are now rejected.

**Error**

[Perform action 'Light: Toggle'
Executed: 5 March 2026 at 07:24:28
Error: extra keys not allowed @ data['color_temp']

Result:
params:
  domain: light
  service: toggle
  service_data:
    entity_id:
      - light.bedroom_ceiling
    brightness_pct: 80
    color_temp: 500
  target: {}
running_script: false](url)

**Example automation action**

```
action:
  - service: light.toggle
    data:
      entity_id: light.bedroom_ceiling
      brightness_pct: 80
      color_temp: 500
```

**Expected behavior**

The action should toggle the light and apply the specified brightness and color temperature, which worked in previous versions.

**Actual behavior**

Home Assistant rejects the action with the error:

extra keys not allowed @ data['color_temp']

**Home Assistant version**

2026.03

**Additional info**

This issue affects multiple automations that previously worked without problems. It appears that light.toggle no longer accepts light attribute parameters such as color_temp or brightness_pct.








### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2026.3.0

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

core-2026.2.4

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

_No response_

### Link to integration documentation on our website

_No response_

### Diagnostics information

_No response_

### Example YAML snippet

```yaml
action:
  - service: light.toggle
    data:
      entity_id: light.bedroom_ceiling
      brightness_pct: 80
      color_temp: 500
```

### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?

```txt

```

### Additional information

_No response_
comments_text: Can be closed. I should have read the changelist.

Honestly, this could be re-opened to add SOMETHING in the app to notify the user about the breaking change. The way things are now a user must have not only reviewed the change-list but also have memorized all the fields in their automations to _just know_ it was going to break.
@WouterJN could you supply a link to the change ? I have the issue also and would like to know what to fix.

comments_text:
Thanks for reporting this, @kyleberry.

The `BadRequest` from b2sdk wasn't being caught specifically, which is why you saw the unhelpful "Unexpected error" message.

I've added proper handling for this in #167482, so you'll now see the actual error message from Backblaze instead of a generic failure.

That said, the root cause appears to be on Backblaze's side.

The b2sdk library calls `b2_authorize_account` on the B2 API v4 endpoint, and the server is rejecting it for your account.

I tested the same endpoint and it works fine on my end, so this seems specific to your account or region.

 To help narrow this down, could you check a few things?
1. What b2sdk version is installed? Your error mentions "API version number 3" but b2sdk 2.10.1 should be using v4 internally, that mismatch is suspicious. From your HA container, can you run pip show b2sdk and share the output?
2. Test the API directly. This will tell us whether Backblaze is rejecting your specific account. From your HA host:  
`curl -X POST https://api.backblazeb2.com/b2api/v3/b2_authorize_account -H "Authorization: Basic $(echo -n 'YOUR_KEY_ID:YOUR_APP_KEY' | base64)"`
3. And the same with /b2api/v4/ instead of /b2api/v3/. (Make sure to redact the response before sharing, I believe it will contain tokens.) 
4. Account and region details. When was your Backblaze account created, and which region is the bucket in (US West, EU Central, etc.)?

Thanks for the quick response @ElCruncharino 

1. I'm running managed HAOS but I exec'd into the `homeassistant` container (I assume this is where `core` runs?):
```bash
homeassistant:/config# pip show b2sdk
Name: b2sdk
Version: 2.10.1
Summary: Backblaze B2 SDK
Home-page: https://github.com/Backblaze/b2-sdk-python
Author:
Author-email: Backblaze Inc <support@backblaze.com>
License: MIT
Location: /usr/local/lib/python3.14/site-packages
Requires: annotated_types, logfury, requests
Required-by:
```
2. I _think_ this is expected since this isn't the master key, testing a v3 endpoint with the master key works fine:
```bash
homeassistant:/config# curl https://api.backblazeb2.com/b2api/v3/b2_authorize_account -u "$KEY_ID:$KEY"
{
  "code": "bad_request",
  "message": "This request is not currently supported on API version number 3",
  "status": 400
}   
```
Same request using the master key:
```bash
homeassistant:/config# curl https://api.backblazeb2.com/b2api/v3/b2_authorize_account -u "$MASTER_KEY_ID:$MASTER_KEY"
{
  "accountId": "2593a7b7b28c",

title: Integral sensor looses data after every HA restart.
body: ### The problem Integral sensors loose data after Home Assistant restart. Happens for both YAML defined and UI created helpers. Also happens to Utility Meter helpers. I recently had to restart HA few times due to testing of unrelated integration (Ecovacs) and noticed messed up data in Energy dashboard. Turns out all of my integration sensors and utility meter helpers reset to values they had right before updating to 2026.3.0 which generates negative numbers in energy statistics. No errors in logs from what I can see. Below you can see a history graph of one of those Integral helpers. Value of this sensor should never drop. Every drop you see on the graph is a drop to a last value it had before updating to 2026.3.0. I rolled back to 2026.2.3 and tested - this does not happen in 2026.2.3 ### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue? core-2026.3.0 ### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core? core-2026.2.3 ### What type of installation are you running? author: grifone1893

title: webostv media_player.turn_on returns HTTP 500 when no turn-on action is configured
body:
## Summary
Calling `media_player.turn_on` on an LG webOS TV entity from Home Assistant returns HTTP 500 instead of a clearer validation/configuration error when no turn-on automation/action is configured.

Based on the integration docs, turn-on requires a separate automation (for example Wake-on-LAN or HDMI-CEC). So this may be expected operationally, but the current behavior looks like a server error rather than a user-facing configuration problem.

## Environment
- Home Assistant integration: `webostv`
- Entity: `media_player.lg_sala`
- TV powers off correctly with `media_player.turn_off`
- `media_player.turn_on` fails consistently with HTTP 500

## What I observed
From an external capability wrapper that calls Home Assistant services, this payload succeeds for off and fails for on:

```json
{
  "domain": "media_player",
  "service": "turn_on",
  "entity_ids": ["media_player.lg_sala"],
  "service_data": {},
  "return_response": false
}
```

Returned error:

```
Home Assistant request failed with HTTP 500: 500 Internal Server Error

Server got itself in trouble
```

After the failed call, the entity state remains `off`.

## Why this seems worth fixing
The docs explicitly say turn-on needs a separate automation trigger/action, so a generic `500 Internal Server Error` is hard to interpret. A more descriptive service error or configuration warning would make this much easier to diagnose.

## Docs reference
https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/webostv/

Relevant section: **Turn on automation trigger**

## Suggestion
If no turn-on action/trigger is configured for the entity, could `media_player.turn_on` fail with a clearer, user-oriented error instead of surfacing as HTTP 500?
comments_text: Correct, should not show a 500 error, I will check it, thanks

I could not reproduce it, the service call is returning the correct status. Your issue is missing the issue template: If you still experiencing this issue, please create a new issue and make sure to fill out all the details and fields, I can't support this issue without the relevant data, thanks for understanding.
